definition: Post-2004 financialization has structurally degraded commodity investable returns through roll yield collapse (from +5.1% to -3.2% annualized), elevated commodity-equity correlation (from +0.03 to +0.48), and $450B+ index AUM suppressing backwardation. Bai-Perron structural break confirmed at 2004Q2 (t-stat 5.1).
evidence: Roll yield collapsed from +5.1% to -3.2% annualized
GSCI spot -10% vs. total return -35% cumulative 2005-2025
Partial reversion to +0.28 correlation statistically insufficient for second regime break
open_questions: Is the partial reversion to +0.28 correlation stable or transitional?
Can active strategies overcome the structural impairment?
